Apply for Credit Card Online

  • Exclusive Rewards for New Cardholders
  • Convert Purchases into Easy EMIs
  • Earn Points, CashBack, Redeem Vouchers & More
Apply Now

What’s In Store For You

  • Types of Credit Cards
  • More About Credit Cards
  • Frequently Asked Questions

Whether you are a frequent traveller, an avid shopper, or someone looking for everyday convenience, HDFC Bank has a Credit Card for you.

 

From daily purchases to special occasions, the right Credit Card can help make every transaction more rewarding while offering benefits, savings and privileges that fit seamlessly into your lifestyle.

Based on your inputs we suggest these cards

You can only select 3 cards for comparison. Kindly remove any one card to add another card.

Select your occupation

₹
₹300000 ₹5000000

See What Our Customers Have to Say​

Join Over 2.5 Crore+ Happy Customers

Hands down one of the best credit cards in my opinion when it comes to cashback. Millennia is my primary card so I have accumulated a significant number of points. I usually convert the points to statement credit to get a reduced card bill. If you don't travel that often, go for the 1k voucher as the milestone benefit.

Sameer Sahu

I was able to get the Swiggy credit card quickly as I was pre-approved. Applied online without having to submit any documents and got a decent limit as well. If you use Swiggy a lot, I'd definitely recommend this card since you also get cashback.

Ankit Badoni

The Pixel Credit Card is great for beginners, and I really like the cashback offers. I use it mostly for dining out due to the massive dineout discounts!

Aryan Vinod

The Millennia Credit card is a good core banking card especially if you're a beginner. I've been using the card for some time now and it's a no-fuss card. You can get cashback as cashpoints and redeem them for vouchers or statement credit. I got in touch with customer care for changing the billing date, they processed the request immediately.

Merwyn Dsouza

If you travel a lot, don't sleep on the Regalia Gold Credit Card. You get lounge access, 2% mark up on forex, and emergency hospitalisation cover for international travel…I book my flights through Smartbuy for additional cashback. Would also recommend activating Global Value Program for 1% cashback on international transactions. Totally worth it.

Ritika Shewani

I dine out quite often so I opted for the Swiggy Credit Card. Application process was surprisingly straightforward. No paperwork required, got a decent limit and also the physical card was delivered within a week. I would definitely recommend!

Yash Lad

One of the best features of the Pixel Credit Card is selecting merchants that I often shop from, I can get more value out of the card. Also, this credit card can be easily managed through PayZapp.

Amatulla Challawala

Got a pre-approved offer for Diners Club privilege recently. Since I was pre-approved, the application process was very quick.

Cynthia Dsouza

The MoneyBack+ Credit Card is great for frequent online shoppers and diners like me. It's a generally good card for everyday expenses. Easy online application process too.

Hetavi Tushar Shah

I got the Tata Neu Credit Card RuPay variant mainly for UPI payments which also gives decent cashback as Neucoins which you can redeem for air travel, hotel booking, etc. I also got my limit upgraded through the customer care easily. Good card, good experience with customer support.

Omkar Shankar Dhurat

Exclusive Credit Card Range for Business and UPI

Credit Cards Against Fixed Deposit

  • Enjoy easy approval with no income proof required.
  • Build your credit score while your fixed deposit keeps growing.
  • Earn interest on your FD while enjoying credit card benefits.
Apply Now Know More

RuPay Credit Cards

  • Versatile & accessible credit solutions for your financial needs.
  • Earn rewards on groceries, dining & everyday spending.
  • Enjoy flexible repayment options with up to 50 days of interest-free period.
Apply Now Know More

Business Credit Cards

  • Tailored Business Credit Cards for flexible credit access.
  • Track, analyse and control your spending through digital platforms.
  • Earn rewards with dining & travel benefits.
Apply Now Know More

Commercial Credit Cards

  • Streamline your finances with robust solutions that simplify business expenses.
  • Higher credit limit for greater purchasing power.
  • Control employee spending, manage vendor payments and earn rewards.
Enquire Now Know More

More About Credit Cards

Click here to access all important links related to your Credit Card.

New Card Holder Benefits

  • Exclusive benefits when you sign up for a Credit Card with HDFC Bank

  • Additional rewards on annual spends above a certain amount

SmartEMI

  • Easy EMIs on Credit Card purchases of ₹2,500 or more 

  • Flexible repayment options

  • Competitive interest rates for online/in-store purchases

Contactless Payments

  • Quick tap payment option for offline transactions 

  • Activate contactless payments on MyCards

  • Set contactless payment limit for complete security

Foreign Currency Markup

  • Competitive foreign currency markup on select HDFC Bank Credit Cards 

  • Savings on international spends

Insurance Coverage

  • Complimentary insurance coverage with select HDFC Bank Credit Cards 

  • Secure air accident and emergency hospitalisation coverage worth lakhs 

Revolving Credit

  • Revolving credit facilities at affordable interest rates with HDFC Bank Credit Cards

  • To know more, review our Most Important Terms and Conditions (MITC)

Airport Lounge Access

  • Free access to airport lounges at both domestic and international airports with select HDFC Bank Credit Cards

No Lost Card Liability

  • All-around support centre to report Credit Card lost/stolen complaints

  • Liability waivers on fraudulent transactions on prompt reporting 

Make UPI Payments

  • Your RuPay Credit Card is enabled for UPI payments. Link your RuPay Credit Card to a UPI-enabled app like PayZapp and make payments instantly

  • Age: At least 18 years

  • Nationality: Indian National

  • Residential Status: Resident/Non-Resident Indian

  • Employment Status: Salaried/Self-employed individual

  • Annual Income: HDFC Bank determines your eligibility for Credit Card and subsequent credit limit based on your annual income.

*HDFC Bank revises your credit limit based on your annual spends, card usage, and income. We also send invitations to upgrade your Credit Cards based on your usage.

You can apply for a new HDFC Bank Credit Card online or by visiting your nearest HDFC Bank branch. To apply online, follow these steps:

  • 1. Go to our website > > Credit Card page.
  • 2. Check your eligibility through the online eligibility calculator.
  • 3. Get the right recommendation for an HDFC Bank Credit Card for you and apply.
  • 4. Get a new Credit Card once your application is approved.

 

Credit Card Statement

HDFC Bank sends a Credit Card statement every month to your registered email address which lists all your transactions. It includes the credit limit you have utilised, the payment due date and the total outstanding balance. The statement is generated as per your billing cycle.

View More

Cash withdrawals with Credit Card

Cash Advance service allows you to withdraw cash from ATMs using your Credit Card. The amount you can withdraw depends on your card type and the predetermined cash advance limit. The bank also levies interest on the withdrawn amount along with other applicable charges.

Read More

Fees & Charges

HDFC Bank charges specific fees on your Credit Card use as per your card category. These fees include annual maintenance charges, fees for cash advances, foreign currency conversion fees, late payment charges, interest on unpaid balances, fees for exceeding your credit limit, applicable Goods and Services Tax (GST) and other charges as applicable.

Read More

Reward Points

Earn rewards and CashBack and enjoy exciting offers on your online and offline Credit Card transactions. You can redeem the accumulated reward points for brand vouchers, air miles, statement credit and more. Ensure to use your reward points before expiry.

Read More

Bill Payment

When the Credit Card bill is generated, you have up to 20 days to pay the amount due. You must pay at least the minimum amount due before the due date to avoid late payment charges. You also have the option to make a part payment. However, the outstanding balance will accrue interest and applicable taxes.

Read More

Dos

  • Find the ideal card for you
    HDFC Bank offers a wide range of Credit Cards to suit every lifestyle. You can choose your preferred Credit Card from our bouquet of offerings, based on your eligibility.

  • Read the Terms & Conditions
    You must check the Terms & Conditions concerning credit limits, interest rates, cash withdrawals, fees, waivers, discounts, Credit Card offers, etc. when applying for a Credit Card online.

  • Repay dues on time
    Repaying dues in full showcases your creditworthiness to the bank leading to a healthy credit score. This lets you enjoy a bunch of benefits and offers. 

  • Benefit from increased credit limit
    HDFC Bank will inform you if you qualify for an increase in your credit limit. You can take advantage of the enhanced credit limit and enjoy additional reward points and offers, as per your spends.

  • Keep your card secure
    Protect your card from theft and your card information from being compromised.

  • Stay informed about payment dates
    The billing date is when the bank sends you an itemised bill comprising their Credit Card spending during the last billing cycle, and the payment date is the date on or before which you must clear your dues. You need to keep track of these dates to avoid late payment charges.

  • Monitor your spends
    You are advised to keep a track of your Credit Card spends to avoid fraudulent transactions. You can do so by opting for SMS and email alerts for every transaction on your Credit Card. Additionally, in case you suspect any fraudulent transactions it is recommended to inform the Bank and promptly block the card.

Don'ts

  • Don’t over utilise the entire credit limit 
    Over-utilisation of the credit limit can impact your credit score and can put an additional financial burden on you, making the repayment difficult. It is advisable to manage credit responsibly to maintain a healthy credit score.

  • Don’t apply for too many Credit Cards
    While it may seem wise to have more Credit Cards and extended credit limits, especially during emergencies, applying for multiple Credit Cards may not be prudent. It can reflect as need for more credit or imply risk-taking tendencies and may impact your credit score.

  • Don’t pay just the minimum due
    Paying only the minimum amount due on your Credit Card is not recommended. By choosing this option, you would typically carry forward the remaining amount to the next cycle, incurring an interest rate on the delayed payment and increasing your overall Credit Card debt.

Understanding how to use a Credit Card wisely is vital. Consider these security tips.

  • Avoid sharing Credit Card details
    Your Credit Card comprises details like your name, card number, expiration date, and CVV number. While making online transactions, you must enter these details. You must also input the 4-digit PIN along with a One Time Password (OTP) the bank sends on your registered mobile number to authenticate the transaction. Ensure secure transactions by not sharing your PIN or OTP with anyone.

  • Be cautious with Overseas usage

    You can activate your card for international usage anytime. We recommend you deactivate it soon after arriving in India. You must inform us immediately upon losing your card or if you notice fraudulent or suspicious transactions on your card. The bank will block the card immediately in such cases.

  • Manage your account details 
    You should always use a strong password for your banking needs. Regularly tracking your account statement is an excellent way to check against unauthorised transactions. If you ever spot any unknown transaction, let your bank know immediately. You can also set transaction limits with your bank to control the extent of loss if you ever become a victim of any fraud.

Explained below are a few types of Credit Card fees you ought to know: 

  • Annual Fee/Renewal Fees: This is the fee that the issuer charges annually to you. The fee can vary across. 

  • Cash Advance Fees: This is applicable when you withdraw cash from an ATM using a Credit Card.  

  • Finance Charges: These are payable at the monthly percentage rate on all transactions if you don’t pay your Credit Card bill in full. 

  • Late Payment Fee: A late payment fee is applicable if you fail to pay your Credit Card bill by the due date.  

  • Foreign Transaction Fee: A transaction fee will be applicable if you use you use your card for international transactions. 

  • Balance Transfer Fee: When you transfer balance from one Credit Card to another, you will incur a transfer fee. 

You can avail of a wide range of Credit Cards in India, including:

CashBack Credit Card

This type of Credit Card offers CashBack on your online and offline spends. Typically, you’ll receive a percentage of the spends amount as CashBack. For instance, the HDFC Bank Millennia Credit Card offers a 5% CashBack on certain e-commerce spends.

View More

Shopping Credit Card

This type of Credit Card offers exclusive rewards on retail spends. These can be co-branded Credit Cards that offer greater perks when you use the card when shopping at the particular brand online and offline.

Read More

Premium & Super Premium Credit Card

The card issuer issues such types of Credit Cards to select individuals. The minimum income criteria to be eligible for the card is relatively high. Also, these cards may come with premium perks such as unlimited airport lounge access or gold course access.

Read More

Co-Branded Credit Card

HDFC Bank has tie-ups with several brands. Through this tie-up, the bank offers Co-branded Credit Cards. When you use the card at the brand outlets, you can receive exclusive rewards or loyalty points.

Read More

Rewards Credit Card

A Rewards Credit Card features an extensive rewards programme where you can earn attractive rewards, in the form of CashBack, reward points or loyalty points.

Read More

Travel Credit Card

This type of Credit Card offers exclusive rewards on travel-related spends. You can earn rewards on the hotel, flight, train, etc., bookings. With certain Cards, you can redeem these rewards to get travel vouchers.

Read More

Airline Credit Card

An airline Credit Card offers special rewards when you make air travel-related spends. For instance, the 6E Rewards XL – IndiGo HDFC Bank Credit Card offers 5% 6E Rewards on IndiGo spends.

Read More

International Credit Card

You can enable international transactions on most Credit Cards. However, certain specific cards may come with lower foreign currency mark-up charges to help you maximise cost-savings. You can use such cards at overseas point-of-sale (POS) terminals, ATMs, etc.

Read More

Lifestyle Credit Card

This type of card is designed to offer special benefits, including rewards, CashBack, travel, health and wellness, dining, etc.

Read More

Selecting the right Credit Card involves evaluating your spending habits, financial goals and the features offered by different cards. Consider the following factors when you apply for a Credit Card: 

  • Identify where you spend the most, whether groceries, travel, shopping, etc.  

  • What are your financial goals? Do you want a card to earn rewards, CashBack, miles, etc.? Or do you simply wish to improve your credit score? 

  • Once you have determined your objective, it’s easier to shortlist a Credit Card type for your needs. You can choose from Rewards Cards, Travel Cards, Shopping Cards, Secured Cards, etc. 

  • Compare the features, benefits and terms of different cards, including annual fees, reward programmes, welcome bonuses, milestone benefits, interest rates and lounge access. 

  • Familiarise yourself with the late payment fees, foreign transaction fees, balance transfer fees, and cash advance fees. Carefully read the terms and conditions, including the interest rate terms, fees, and rewards program rules.

*The Most Important Terms and Conditions for each of our banking offerings features all the specific terms and conditions that govern their use. You must go through it thoroughly to fully understand the terms and conditions applicable to any banking product you choose.

Frequently Asked Questions

Credit Cards are financial tools that allow you to make purchases while paying for them later. It comes with a predetermined credit limit that you can use to make online or in-store purchases. You receive a monthly statement detailing your spending that needs to be paid within a specific period. 

A Credit Card allows you to buy products and services on credit, while you can pay for the same later as per your settlement date. It enables you to make purchases instantly by extending you a line of credit.

When you use a Credit Card for a transaction, the bank pays the merchants or retailers on your behalf and you have to repay the amount to the bank as per your settlement period. Each month, you receive a statement detailing your dues. You have the option to pay the full balance or a minimum payment on or before the Credit Card due date. You also receive reward points and Credit Card offers based on your transactions.

You can easily check your eligibility by visiting the HDFC Bank Credit Card page on the website. Enter your mobile number and date of birth, and we will let you know if you are eligible to get an HDFC Bank Credit Card.

For new customers:

  1. Income proof
  2. Identity proof
  3. Address proof

For existing HDFC Bank customers:

  1. Income proof: Salary slips/income tax returns for the last three years.
  2. Identity proof: Not needed.
  3. Address proof: Not needed.

    [Since your KYC and address documents are already updated in our records, they are not needed. If we do need your address proof, we will let you know. In case your latest mailing address requires changing, you need to provide your latest address proof and income proof. Acceptable mailing address proof documents include an Aadhaar card/ Indian passport/ Voter ID (issued in India) / Driving License (issued in India).

You can easily apply for an HDFC Bank Credit Card despite already having a Credit Card if:

  1. Your existing Credit Card is from another bank
  2. You have a good credit score 

However, if you already have an HDFC Bank Credit Card, we will not be able to give you another HDFC Bank Credit Card.

Visit the HDFC Bank MyCards portal to effortlessly monitor and manage your Credit Card expenses.

Easily monitor and control your Credit Card Spending through HDFC Bank MyCards.

HDFC Bank Credit Cards come with certain payable charges irrespective of whether you use your card. However, if you meet certain conditions, we can waive those charges. Click here to know the conditions mentioned above.

Once you have accumulated reward points, you can redeem them for products through the rewards catalogue, gift vouchers, flight tickets, hotel bookings, etc., through NetBanking.

You don't have to pay interest on purchases. However, if you are unable to pay the total outstanding amount on or before the bill due date, the unpaid dues and all additional transactions attract an interest penalty till the pending amount is repaid in full.

If you do not pay the minimum amount due on your Credit Card by the payment due date, you will be levied a late payment fee. Please note that frequent late payments may affect your credit score.

You can convert your Credit Card bills into monthly EMIs via SmartEMIs by checking your eligibility for via NetBanking. You can get started here.

A Credit Card balance transfer allows you to move your outstanding Credit Card dues from one card to another. This can be with a card offering a lower interest rate or a limited-period zero-interest offer.

A Credit Card balance transfer can help manage debt by potentially reducing interest costs, freeing up monthly cash flow and making it easier to pay off the debt faster. Click here to know more.

You can pay your Credit Card bills via:

  1. PayZapp

  2. NetBanking

  3. Mobile Banking

  4. ATM fund transfer

  5. Cash

  6. Cheque/Demand Draft
